items.title: USDA finalizes tougher SNAP retailer stocking standards to expand healthy food options
items.summary: USDA published a final rule requiring SNAP-authorized retailers to carry seven varieties of staple foods across four categories (protein, grains, dairy, and fruits/vegetables). The agency says the new standard more than doubles the prior requirement and is scheduled to take effect in Fall 2026.

items.title: CMS issues interim final rule implementing Medicaid work requirement for certain adults
items.summary: CMS issued an interim final rule implementing a new “community engagement” requirement: certain non-pregnant adults ages 19–64 generally must complete 80 hours per month of qualifying activities (work, job programs, community service, or half-time education). States must generally implement the requirement by January 1, 2027, and the rule also allows meeting it through monthly earnings of at least 80 times the federal minimum wage (listed as $580/month in 2026).

items.title: IRS posts updated EITC tables: maximum credit up to $8,046 for tax year 2025
items.summary: The IRS EITC tables list maximum credits for tax year 2025 of $649 (no qualifying children), $4,328 (one child), $7,152 (two children), and $8,046 (three or more children). The page also notes an investment income limit of $11,950 or less to qualify.

items.title: IRS: Child Tax Credit up to $2,200 per child; refundable portion up to $1,700 (if eligible)
items.summary: The IRS Child Tax Credit page states the credit is worth up to $2,200 per qualifying child. It also says families with little or no federal income tax liability may qualify for the Additional Child Tax Credit (refundable portion) up to $1,700 per qualifying child, and notes an earned income threshold of at least $2,500 to be eligible.

items.title: Federal Register posts 2026–27 award-year FAFSA processing receipt deadline: Aug. 31, 2027
items.summary: A Federal Register notice posted by Federal Student Aid lists August 31, 2027 as the final FAFSA processing receipt deadline for the 2026–27 award year. The notice also lists June 30, 2027 and September 29, 2027 as additional 2026–27 award-year deadlines for reports/records across programs including Pell Grants, Direct Loans, FSEOG, and Federal Work-Study.

items.title: HUD: $193M available for youth homelessness programs; applications due Aug. 10, 2026
items.summary: HUD states it is competing $144 million for the Youth Homelessness Demonstration Program (YHDP) to fund 26–50 new communities, plus $49 million for Youth Homelessness System Improvement (YHSI) grants. HUD lists an application deadline of August 10, 2026.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: SSA publishes 2026–2027 benefit payment schedule (SSI generally paid on the 1st)
items.summary: SSA’s 2026–2027 payment schedule explains that SSI is generally paid on the 1st of each month, while Social Security is paid on the 3rd for people who got benefits before May 1997 or who receive both Social Security and SSI. The publication also advises allowing three additional mailing days after the expected date before contacting SSA if a payment isn’t received.
